lowpower_init.message0: Initialize low power mode CPU freq %1 power mode %2
lowpower_init.tooltip: Initialize low power mode, set CPU frequency and power latency mode. Low Power allows clocks to gate during idle; Constant Latency keeps clocks running for faster wake response.

lowpower_enter_lowest_power.tooltip: Shut down board-level power rails and shared control nets for lowest power state. Typically called before System OFF.

lowpower_set_cpu_freq.tooltip: Dynamically switch CPU frequency at runtime. 64 MHz for low power, 128 MHz for high performance.

lowpower_wfi.tooltip: CPU enters WFI (Wait For Interrupt) idle state until next interrupt. Simplest low power method in System ON.

lowpower_sleep_ms.tooltip: Low power delay using WFI loop, CPU automatically enters idle during wait. More power efficient than delay().

lowpower_system_off_timed.tooltip: Enter deepest System OFF sleep, cold boot wake after specified time. Execution restarts from setup(). Lowest power timed wake method.

lowpower_system_off_button.tooltip: Configure user button as wake source, then enter deepest System OFF sleep. Press button to cold boot wake, execution restarts from setup().

lowpower_system_off.tooltip: Enter deepest System OFF sleep mode. Requires pre-configured wake source (e.g. LPCOMP). No retention = lowest power, .noinit retention preserves small data across sleep.

lowpower_lpcomp_init.message0: Init LPCOMP pin %1 VDD %2 mV threshold %3 mV hysteresis %4 detect %5
lowpower_lpcomp_init.tooltip: Initialize Low Power Comparator (LPCOMP) to monitor analog pin voltage crossing a threshold. LPCOMP works while CPU sleeps, can be used as System OFF wake source. VDD is supply voltage (USB: 3300, LiPo: 3700).

lowpower_lpcomp_poll_up.tooltip: Check if LPCOMP detected voltage rising above threshold. Event flag auto-cleared after read. Suitable for polling in loop().

lowpower_lpcomp_sample_above.tooltip: Immediately sample LPCOMP comparator, returns whether analog input is currently above threshold.

lowpower_lpcomp_clear.tooltip: Clear all LPCOMP event flags to prevent false triggers. Typically called after LPCOMP initialization.

lowpower_watchdog_init.message0: Init watchdog timeout %1 ms pause in sleep %2
lowpower_watchdog_init.tooltip: Initialize watchdog timer. If not fed within timeout, the chip resets automatically. When pause in sleep is enabled, watchdog pauses during CPU sleep.

lowpower_watchdog_feed.tooltip: Reset watchdog timer to prevent system reset. Must be called periodically before timeout expires.

lowpower_woke_from.tooltip: Check if this boot was triggered by the specified wake source. Use in setup() to determine cold boot vs. sleep wake. Must be used before clearing reset reason.

lowpower_reset_reason.tooltip: Get raw value of reset reason register for advanced debugging. Must be read before clearing reset reason.

lowpower_clear_reset_reason.tooltip: Clear reset reason register. Typically called after reading and handling wake source to prevent misidentification on next boot.

lowpower_gpregret_write.tooltip: Write a byte value (0-255) to GPREGRET retained register. Value is preserved during System OFF, useful for marking sleep state or passing info to post-wake program.

lowpower_gpregret_read.tooltip: Read a byte value (0-255) from GPREGRET retained register. Value is preserved during System OFF, useful for detecting wake from sleep.
